Security best practices for building sec: Security best practices for building secure applications with MBC CQRS Serverless.
This guide covers security best practice: This guide covers security best practices for building secure applications with MBC CQRS Serverless, including input validation, authentication, authorization, and data protection.
Always validate input at the API boundar: Always validate input at the API boundary using class-validator decorators.
Prevent XSS and injection attacks by san: Prevent XSS and injection attacks by sanitizing string input.
Restrict file types, sizes, and scan for: Restrict file types, sizes, and scan for malware.
Use strong password policies and MFA.
CDK configuration for Cognito User Pool: CDK configuration for Cognito User Pool
The default implementation uses `minLeng: The default implementation uses `minLength: 6` for development convenience. For production environments, we strongly recommend using at least 12 characters as shown above, along with MFA enabled.
Always validate JWT tokens on the server: Always validate JWT tokens on the server side.
Implement secure token refresh without s: Implement secure token refresh without storing refresh tokens on client.
Use the framework's `@Auth` decorator, w: Use the framework's `@Auth` decorator, which applies `RolesGuard` and checks the roles carried in the `custom:roles` JWT claim (and group-derived roles from `custom:groups`). See [Authentication](/docs/authentication) for the full role model.
Only users holding the required role can: Only users holding the required role can access
Always derive the tenant code from the v: Always derive the tenant code from the verified JWT via `getUserContext()` — never from request parameters or the request body. The framework's `RolesGuard` verifies tenant access (including the `x-tenant-code` header override for common tenants).
The tenant code comes from the verified: The tenant code comes from the verified token, not from user input
Tenant is always part of the partition k: Tenant is always part of the partition key
Derive user identity from verified JWT —: Derive user identity from verified JWT — never from request parameters
Create a logger that masks sensitive fie: Create a logger that masks sensitive fields
Use AWS Secrets Manager for sensitive co: Use AWS Secrets Manager for sensitive configuration.
Cache secrets to avoid repeated API call: Cache secrets to avoid repeated API calls
Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse: Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse.
The default implementation uses `allowed: The default implementation uses `allowedOrigins: ['*']` for development convenience. For production environments, you should always restrict allowed origins to specific domains as shown above to prevent cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks.
Limit request payload size to prevent Do: Limit request payload size to prevent DoS.
API Gateway payload limit in serverless.yml
Grant only necessary permissions to Lamb: Grant only necessary permissions to Lambda functions.
Deploy Lambda functions in VPC for datab: Deploy Lambda functions in VPC for database access.
Log all API calls for audit.
All input is validated with class-valida: All input is validated with class-validator
Sensitive data is encrypted at rest: Sensitive data is encrypted at rest
Secrets are stored in Secrets Manager: Secrets are stored in Secrets Manager
VPC is configured for database access: VPC is configured for database access
In a service class with dataService and: In a service class with dataService and commandService injected
Check ownership — allow system_admin to: Check ownership — allow system_admin to bypass
S3 access - specific bucket and prefix: S3 access - specific bucket and prefix
When implementing group-based role resol: When implementing group-based role resolution (v1.3.1+), **do not annotate your resolver class with both `@GroupRoleResolver()` and `@Injectable()`**. `@GroupRoleResolver()` already registers the class as a singleton provider — adding `@Injectable()` creates a conflicting second registration that causes a NestJS DI error at startup.
See [Group-Based Roles](/docs/authentica: See [Group-Based Roles](/docs/authentication#group-based-roles) for implementation details and [Common Issues](/docs/common-issues#authentication-errors) for troubleshooting.
